truth.papers.A42.title: A modified Johnson-Cook model to determine plastic flow behavior of Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.8 C low-density steel during warm multiaxial forging
truth.papers.A42.paper_scope: Investigate microstructural evolution and strengthening mechanisms of Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.8C low-density steel during multiaxial forging at 250°C, and develop a modified Johnson-Cook constitutive model incorporating dislocation density and grain size effects to predict flow stress and yield strength via finite element simulation.

truth.alloys.alloy_30mn9al08c.alloys_notes: Actual composition after solutionization: Fe-30.6Mn-9.1Al-0.79C. Density of steel measured as 6.8 g/cm³.

truth.processes.proc_maf.description: Multiaxial forging (MAF) at 250°C with 33% normal strain per pass, up to 5 passes. Samples were heated to 250°C and soaked for 2 hours before each pass. Compressed in three mutually perpendicular directions per pass.
truth.processes.proc_maf.processes_notes: MAF conducted on a 100 ton hydraulic press, ram speed 1 mm/s. Lubricated with MoS2. Prior to MAF, ingot homogenized at 1200°C for 2h, hot forged between 1200-900°C, then solutionized at 1050°C for 1h and water quenched.

truth.processing_steps.sample_maf0.processing_: Vacuum induction melted, homogenized at 1200°C 2h, hot forged between 1200-900°C with >60% deformation, solutionized at 1050°C 1h, water quenched. No MAF pass applied.
truth.processing_steps.sample_maf1.processing_: Sample heated to 250°C and soaked 2h; compressed in three mutually perpendicular directions; ram speed 1 mm/s.

truth.structures.sample_maf0.overall_structure: Single-phase austenitic matrix with coarse grains and low dislocation density.
truth.structures.sample_maf0.microstructure_li: Strong texture; significant fraction of grains have [101] parallel to pressing direction and some [111] oriented.
Strong texture with [101]//pressing direction; high angle grain boundary fraction 0.62.
truth.structures.sample_maf1.overall_structure: Single-phase austenitic matrix with refined grains and increased dislocation density.
truth.structures.sample_maf1.microstructure_li: Texture begins to randomize; multiple orientations appear parallel to pressing direction.
High angle grain boundary fraction decreases to 0.29; low angle fraction increases to 0.70.
truth.structures.sample_maf3.overall_structure: Single-phase austenitic matrix with further grain refinement and high dislocation density.
truth.structures.sample_maf3.microstructure_li: refined grains, mixed with some lamellar features
High angle boundary fraction 0.24; low angle fraction 0.75.
truth.structures.sample_maf5.overall_structure: Single-phase austenitic matrix with lamellar refined grains (transverse dimension ~13 μm) and very high dislocation density.
truth.structures.sample_maf5.microstructure_li: lamellar grains with transverse dimension about 13 μm
Significantly randomized texture; originally strong [101] texture weakened.
High angle boundary fraction recovers to 0.69; low angle 0.30; some low-angle boundaries converted to high-angle.

truth.properties.sample_maf0.mechanical.tensil: Engineering stress-strain curve shown in Fig. 10; exhibits high work hardening.

truth.characterization_methods.sample_maf0.pur: Determine mechanical properties after 0 pass MAF.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_maf0.key: YS 380 MPa, UTS 762 MPa, total elongation 75%, uniform elongation 63%; high work hardening.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_maf1.key: YS 1040 MPa, UTS 1368 MPa, total elongation 30%.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_maf3.key: YS 1279 MPa, UTS 1415 MPa, total elongation 13.7%, uniform elongation 3%.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_maf5.key: YS 1528 MPa, UTS 1548 MPa, total elongation 8%, uniform elongation 0.8%.

truth.computational_details.idx_0.simulation_m: Finite element method (ABAQUS/Explicit) with VUMAT subroutine incorporating modified Johnson-Cook constitutive model that includes evolution of dislocation density (Eq. 7) and grain size (Eq. 8) as a function of equivalent plastic strain.
truth.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Predicted yield strength, dislocation density, and grain size distributions compared with experimental results; good agreement up to 3 passes, slight underprediction at 5 passes attributed to neglecting texture effects.
truth.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Flow stress constitutive equation incorporating grain boundary strengthening (Hall-Petch) and dislocation strengthening (Taylor equation) into yield stress.
Implemented via VUMAT in ABAQUS; SDVs track equivalent plastic strain, strain rate, dislocation density, flow stress, yield strength, grain size.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Manganese added under 600 mbar argon pressure during vacuum induction melting.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: The steel could be forged only up to five passes before fracture on the sixth attempt.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: No deformation twinning observed during MAF; negligible annealing twin fraction (0.007 in MAF-1, 0.003 in MAF-5).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Initial grain size distribution wide, became narrower and more uniform after 5 passes.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Texture: MAF-0 strongly textured with [101] parallel to pressing direction; randomized with increasing passes.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Crystallite size reduced from 61 nm to 10.12 nm (Table 1).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_10.value: Work hardening amount (UTS minus YS): MAF-0 392 MPa, MAF-1 328 MPa, MAF-3 126 MPa, MAF-5 20 MPa.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_11.value: Peak shift in XRD indicates texturing: from (111) dominant in MAF-0 to (220) in MAF-1.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_12.value: Hall-Petch constant k for grain boundary strengthening: 2374 MPa·μm^0.5.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_13.value: Taylor constant α=0.24, average Taylor factor M=3.0, shear modulus G=61 GPa, Burgers vector b=0.259 nm.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_14.value: Friction coefficient between workpiece and die: 0.15.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_15.value: Mesh size 0.6 mm, element type C3D8R with 41382 elements, reduced integration and hourglass control.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_16.value: Discrepancy in YS at 3 passes: text says 1279 MPa, Table 2 says 1289 MPa.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_17.value: Predicted flow stress contour maps show higher stress in central region.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_18.value: The simulation does not account for crystallographic texture, leading to underprediction of yield strength at higher passes.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_19.value: Equivalent strain in one MAF pass calculated as 0.47.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_20.value: Strain rate sensitivity C=0.0067, thermal softening exponent m=0.87.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_22.value: The sample and die were heated to 250°C and soaked for 2 hours before MAF.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_23.value: Ram speed during MAF was 1 mm/s.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_24.value: Total accumulated equivalent strains: 0, 0.47, 1.4, and 2.34 for 0, 1, 3, and 5 passes respectively.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_25.value: Dislocation density evolution fitted with Eq. 7: ρ_f = ρ_0 + k1(1-exp(-k2 ε)), with k1=7.9×10^15 m^{-2}, k2=1.813.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_26.value: Grain size evolution fitted with Eq. 8: d_f = d_0 exp(-k3 ε), with k3=0.639.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_27.value: Tensile samples were miniature round specimens with gauge dimensions per Fig. 3(b); tested at strain rate 1×10^{-3} s^{-1} at room temperature.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_28.value: XRD scan parameters: Co-Kα radiation, scan speed 2°/min, step size 0.02°, 2θ range 40°–120°; instrumental broadening corrected with standard silicon.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_29.value: Electro-polishing performed before XRD and EBSD measurements.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_30.value: Yield strength contribution from dislocation density dominated in early passes (1–3), while grain boundary strengthening became more significant in later passes (3–5).
